user_query: I can't decide between the three options for Father's lunch on Day 1. Which one has the highest protein?

selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 3 > Father > Day 1 > Lunch > Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate

node_data.name: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate
node_data.short_description: Marinated grilled chicken skewers served with fluffy couscous, a crisp cucumber-tomato salad, and creamy tzatziki — a classic Greek lunch plate.
node_data.meal_suggestion: Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic for at least 30 minutes. Thread onto skewers and grill until charred. Serve alongside fluffy couscous, a salad of diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and kalamata olives, and a generous dollop of tzatziki. Add a lemon wedge and warm pita on the side.
node_data.nutrition_note: Lean chicken breast is high in protein and low in saturated fat; couscous provides quick-digesting carbohydrates; cucumber and tomato add hydration, vitamins C and K; olives supply monounsaturated fats; tzatziki (yogurt-based) contributes calcium and probiotics. A balanced Mediterranean plate with protein, carbs, healthy fats, and vegetables.

selected_nodes.reasoning: The Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate features lean chicken breast as the primary ingredient, which is explicitly noted as being high in protein and typically contains more protein per serving than lentils or spinach-feta mixtures.

selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 3 > Father > Day 1 > Lunch > Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad

node_data.name: Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad
node_data.short_description: A hearty salad of green lentils tossed with sun-dried tomatoes, feta, arugula, and a bright lemon-herb vinaigrette — plant-powered and filling.
node_data.meal_suggestion: Cook green lentils until tender but not mushy; drain and cool slightly. Toss with chopped sun-dried tomatoes, crumbled feta, baby arugula, diced cucumber, and fresh dill. Dress with a vinaigrette of olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, and minced garlic. Serve at room temperature; keeps well for meal prep.
node_data.nutrition_note: Lentils are an excellent source of plant-based protein, fiber, iron, and folate; sun-dried tomatoes concentrate lycopene and add umami; feta provides calcium and tangy flavor; arugula offers vitamins A, C, and K. Olive oil and lemon create a heart-healthy dressing. High in fiber for digestive health and blood sugar management.

selected_nodes.reasoning: The Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad is a candidate for the protein comparison as lentils are described as an excellent source of plant-based protein.

selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 3 > Father > Day 1 > Lunch > Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita

node_data.name: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita
node_data.short_description: Warm pita pockets filled with a savory spinach-feta mixture inspired by Greek spanakopita — handheld, flavorful, and satisfying.
node_data.meal_suggestion: Sauté fresh spinach with diced onion and garlic in olive oil until wilted. Mix with crumbled feta, a squeeze of lemon, dill, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stuff into warmed whole-wheat pita halves. Serve with a side of Greek salad or hummus and veggie sticks for a complete lunch.
node_data.nutrition_note: Spinach is rich in iron, folate, and vitamins A and K; feta adds protein and calcium; whole-wheat pita provides fiber and complex carbohydrates. Olive oil contributes monounsaturated fats for heart health. A lighter take on traditional spanakopita without the phyllo pastry, reducing saturated fat while keeping all the classic flavors.

selected_nodes.reasoning: The Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita is the third option for Father's lunch on Day 1 mentioned in the query and is needed to facilitate the comparison of protein content.
